stemHtml: How are the air masses distributed in a cold occlusion?

options.a: The coldest air in front of and the warm air behind the occlusion; the less cold air is above ground level.
options.b: The coldest air behind and the warm air in front of the occlusion; the less cold air mass is above ground level.
options.c: The coldest air mass behind and the less cold air in front of the occlusion; the warm air mass is above ground level.
options.d: The coldest air in front of and the less cold air is behind the occlusion; the warm air mass is above ground level.
